channel.title: Jose Fresco Blog
channel.description: Insights and reflections on building web applications, browser extensions, and automation tools

item.title: People Also Ask Miner: A Zero-Dependency Node.js Tool for Content Research
item.description: A lightweight Node.js server that extracts People Also Ask questions from Google search results via SerpApi — zero npm dependencies, a clean browser UI with one-click copy, a CORS proxy for standalone use, and a Windows batch launcher. Covers the server architecture, CORS proxy, frontend copy UX, Windows launcher, the SerpApi vs. direct-scraping tradeoff, and the zero-dependency constraint that keeps setup to a single node command.

item.title: Inside JF Notify: How a WordPress Plugin Hooks into Gravity Forms and Fires Telegram Alerts
item.description: A technical deep-dive into the JF Notify plugin — PHP action hooks, Gravity Forms entry data, Telegram Bot API message formatting, per-form enable/disable toggles, MarkdownV2 escaping, multipart field handling, and the credential filter pattern that keeps bot tokens out of the WordPress database for teams with stricter security requirements.

item.title: Deploying Watch List on Vercel: TMDB Setup, Environment Variables, and PWA Installation
item.description: A step-by-step guide to deploying the Watch List PWA on Vercel — covering TMDB API credentials, the non-obvious three-step session ID flow, build-time credential injection via build.js, configuring MY_SERVICES and EXCLUDED_PROVIDERS streaming filters, and installing the app as a native PWA on iOS, Android, and desktop.

item.title: Encrypted Offsite Backups: Adding GPG and Restore Verification to Pi Backups
item.description: How the pi-backups April 2026 update adds AES-256 GPG encryption of backup archives and an automated restore verification script — protecting Pi application data, configs, and credentials even from your own hosting provider. Covers symmetric vs asymmetric GPG, the updated backup script, the new verify.sh script, weekly cron scheduling, raspberry-pi-agent integration, and a migration guide from the original rsync-based setup.

item.title: Arpy Assist Becomes a Coding Agent: Claude Code, Autonomous Commits, and a Pi That Manages Its Own Repos
item.description: How Arpy Assist evolved from a Qwen CLI web interface into a full Claude Code-powered coding agent on Raspberry Pi — autonomous git workflows, command dispatching via raspberry-pi-agent, and repo management from any browser. Covers the task runner architecture, workspace setup, Claude Code invocation, git commit flow, smart button system, and security model.

item.title: Watch List Internals: Single-File PWA Architecture, Build-Time Credential Injection, and TMDB Session Auth
item.description: A technical deep-dive into how Watch List works under the hood — a single-file PWA with build-time secret injection via build.js, TMDB session authentication for persistent watchlists, and configurable streaming provider filters including MY_SERVICES and EXCLUDED_PROVIDERS constants.

item.title: Building a Fast Developer Portfolio: Critical CSS, Service Workers, and Why I Skipped Frameworks
item.description: How the josefresco.github.io portfolio achieves fast load times through inlined critical CSS, deferred scripts, a service worker cache, and Schema.org structured data — with no framework, no build pipeline, and no bundler in sight.

item.title: Family Dashboard v3.30: Extreme Weather Alerts, Temperature Shifts, and Birthday Detection
item.description: How Family Dashboard grew from v3.26 to v3.30 with extreme weather overlays, temperature shift alerts, multi-account CalDAV, birthday/holiday detection, and a 57% bundle size reduction — all running on a retired iPad as a 24/7 wall display.

item.title: Inside Save to My Blog: Chrome Manifest V3, Service Workers, and the WordPress REST API
item.description: A technical deep-dive into how Save to My Blog works under the hood — Chrome Manifest V3 service workers, popup-to-worker message passing, WordPress Application Password auth, and the lessons learned building a zero-friction content curation tool.

item.title: Running Family Dashboard: From Clone to Kitchen Wall in One Afternoon
item.description: A complete setup guide for Family Dashboard — cloning the repo, configuring OpenWeather and CalDAV credentials, deploying the Vercel serverless proxy, and getting it running as a full-screen kiosk on an iPad.
